meaning: n.澶氬埄瀹夊紡adj.澶氬埄瀹夊紡
examples: The 5th century BC Doric Temple of Apollo at the site of Ancient Corinth.
To the left is an open-plan living and dining space, which is divided by Doric columns.
The Doric order is characterized by the series of triglyphs and metopes on the entablature.
Built in 1803 and fronted by Doric columns, the structure was originally designed to house military orphans.
At least eight Doric temples still stand roofless but erect in Sicily, and six others in spectacular ruins.
